title: Avoid hardcoding endpoints in the TF module
summary: The top-level product Terraform module's terraform/outputs.tf hardcodes most of the coordinator charm's integration-endpoint names as string literals in its `provides` and `requires` outputs, instead of referencing the coordinator sub-module's own `provides`/`requires` outputs. The sub-module coordinator/terraform/outputs.tf already exposes every one of these endpoint names. If someone updates an endpoint name in the coordinator module but forgets to update the duplicated literal in the top-level module, the two drift apart silently and produce broken integrations. The fix is to wire the top-level outputs to module.tempo_coordinator.provides.* / .requires.* (the file already does exactly this for provide_cmr_mesh, require_cmr_mesh and service_mesh, so the pattern is established).
why_good_first_issue: Small, mechanical, well-scoped Terraform-only change touching a single file with an unambiguous done state and zero charm/Python/architecture knowledge required. The correct target values already exist in coordinator/terraform/outputs.tf and the desired pattern is already demonstrated in the same file (lines 23, 37-38), so the contributor just replaces literals with module references. Labelled 'good first issue' by maintainers.
repro_steps: multipass exec cos -- bash -lc 'git clone --depth 80 https://github.com/canonical/tempo-operators ~/work/tempo-operators'; cat -n terraform/outputs.tf  (see hardcoded literals in provides/requires); cat -n coordinator/terraform/outputs.tf  (see the same endpoint names already exposed as outputs); grep -n 'module "tempo_coordinator"' -A1 terraform/main.tf  (confirms source = ../coordinator/terraform).
repro_result: confirmed-by-inspection. terraform/outputs.tf hardcodes endpoint strings: in `provides`, line 19 tempo_cluster="tempo-cluster", line 20 grafana_dashboard="grafana-dashboard", line 21 grafana_source="grafana-source", line 22 metrics_endpoint="metrics-endpoint", line 24 tracing="tracing"; in `requires`, lines 31-36 hardcode logging/ingress/certificates/send-remote-write/receive-datasource/catalogue. coordinator/terraform/outputs.tf ALREADY exports all of these: provides{} (lines 7-12) contains grafana_dashboard, grafana_source, metrics_endpoint, provide_cmr_mesh, tempo_cluster, tracing; requires{} (lines 17-28) contains certificates, ingress, logging, send_remote_write, receive_datasource, require_cmr_mesh, service_mesh, catalogue. Proof the maintainers already use the non-hardcoded pattern: terraform/outputs.tf line 23 uses module.tempo_coordinator.provides.provide_cmr_mesh and lines 37-38 use module.tempo_coordinator.requires.require_cmr_mesh / .service_mesh. So the duplicated literals are exactly the drift risk the issue describes.
fix_hint: In terraform/outputs.tf, replace the hardcoded string literals in `provides` and `requires` with references to the coordinator sub-module outputs, e.g. tempo_cluster = module.tempo_coordinator.provides.tempo_cluster, grafana_dashboard = module.tempo_coordinator.provides.grafana_dashboard, ... and logging = module.tempo_coordinator.requires.logging, ingress = module.tempo_coordinator.requires.ingress, etc. Mind the key-name spelling difference: top-level uses send-remote-write/receive-datasource (hyphens) while the sub-module output keys are send_remote_write/receive_datasource (underscores) -- map them accordingly. Run `terraform fmt` and `terraform validate` in terraform/ if a TF toolchain is available.
notes: Inspection-tier (Terraform, no unit env to run). One first-time contributor (vraj2131) asked to be assigned in a comment but there is no assignee shown and the issue is still open + 'good first issue'; note possible soft-claim. Of the 23 open issues, the cleanest beginner picks are #324 and #257; #272 (Wrong LogQL) is also good-first-issue but is already assigned and being worked on by vraj2131, so it was deliberately not picked.

title: Annoying tracing lib warning: charm tracing buffer exceeds max history length (100 events)
summary: The deprecated charm_tracing charm library logs a WARNING ('charm tracing buffer exceeds max history length (%d events)') every time its in-memory span buffer overflows past max_event_history_length. On charms that ship this lib but run WITHOUT a COS tracing relation (the common case), the buffer fills up over normal operation and emits this WARNING repeatedly into juju-debug-log, creating log noise for an entirely benign/expected condition. Reported against the PostgreSQL charm. The maintainer agreed the level should be lowered and that a new lib revision can be released.
why_good_first_issue: Single-line severity change in one well-isolated function (_prune) with a clear definition of done (no more spurious WARNING; lower level such as info/debug). Requires no architecture knowledge -- it is a logging-level adjustment plus the routine LIBPATCH bump that all ops charmlib edits require. Maintainer pre-approved the direction and a revision release in the issue comments. Labelled 'good first issue'.
repro_steps: multipass exec cos -- bash -lc 'git clone --depth 80 https://github.com/canonical/tempo-operators ~/work/tempo-operators'; grep -rn 'buffer exceeds max history length' --include=*.py .  (locate the log call); sed -n '210,235p' coordinator/lib/charms/tempo_coordinator_k8s/v0/charm_tracing.py  (see logger.warning in _prune); grep -n 'LIBPATCH\|deprecated' coordinator/lib/charms/tempo_coordinator_k8s/v0/charm_tracing.py  (current revision + deprecation).
repro_result: confirmed-by-inspection. The offending call is in _prune() at coordinator/lib/charms/tempo_coordinator_k8s/v0/charm_tracing.py lines 225-228: when overflow > 0 it executes logger.warning("charm tracing buffer exceeds max history length (%d events)", self._max_event_history_length). This is the exact string from the bug report and fires on every prune once the buffer exceeds the default 100-event history, which is normal when no tracing backend is consuming spans. Library metadata: LIBAPI=0, LIBPATCH=13; the file is explicitly marked deprecated ('this library is deprecated and will be discontinued in 27.04', line 7) but the maintainer comment on the issue confirms a new revision can still be cut for this.
fix_hint: Lower the severity of the buffer-overflow log in _prune (charm_tracing.py ~line 225) from logger.warning(...) to logger.info(...) or logger.debug(...) -- debug matches the surrounding dev_logger.debug style used elsewhere in the buffer code. Then bump LIBPATCH from 13 to 14 (do not change LIBAPI). Note the lib stays deprecated; the maintainer suggested new code should migrate to ops[tracing], so scope this PR strictly to the level change + LIBPATCH bump. The charmlib is vendored from tempo_coordinator_k8s, so the canonical source for the edit is this coordinator/ copy.
notes: Inspection-tier: the change is a one-line log-level edit in a charmlib; running the unit suite is not needed to evidence it (the gate that matters for charmlib edits is the LIBPATCH bump, which a contributor must include). The warning originates in the tempo_coordinator_k8s charm_tracing lib but the user-visible symptom appears in any charm vendoring it (e.g. PostgreSQL). Maintainer (PietroPasotti) explicitly OK'd lowering the level and releasing a new revision.
